event: full recovery by resolution of symptoms
received first dose of inactivated SARS-CoV-2 vaccine
received second dose of inactivated SARS-CoV-2 vaccine
took one dose of an antibiotic
SARS-CoV-2 IgG strongly positive at 345 AU/mL
throat swab negative for group A streptococcus
urinalysis showed significant proteinuria of more than 300 µg
ANA, dsDNA, c-ANCA and p-ANCA titres were all negative
faint erythematous non-itchy rash over torso
no pain or swelling of his joints
no consumption of raw dairy products
peripheral lymph nodes were not enlarged
given intravenous hydrocortisone 100 mg two times per day
difficult to mobilise without the support of a walking frame
ECG showed sinus tachycardia with non-specific T-wave abnormalities
transthoracic echocardiogram showed severe tricuspid regurgitation
pulmonary hypertension, PASP of 46 mm Hg
right atrium and ventricle were moderately dilated
left ventricle cavity size was normal with mildly reduced ejection fraction of 45%
computed tomography scan of the chest was negative for pulmonary embolism
patient met criteria for multisystem inflammatory syndrome in adults
started therapy with intravenous steroids, dexamethasone 6 mg daily
condition improved markedly after initiation of dexamethasone
inflammatory markers came down to normal levels
repeat transthoracic echocardiography showed trace tricuspid regurgitation
marked improvement in right ventricular systolic function
tapering dose of prednisolone over a period of 2 weeks
seen in the outpatient clinic 2 weeks following discharge from the hospital
patient’s symptoms resolved although some general weakness and fatigue
